description: Primary care progress note — hypertension and type 2 diabetes management, established patient quarterly follow-up
parsed_text:
PROGRESS NOTE
Great Plains Family Medicine
Department: Primary Care
Encounter ID: ENC-2025-0091847
Date of Service: 2025-10-22

Patient: Anderson, James R.
DOB: 1961-08-05
MRN: GP-MRN-00334712

Provider: Dr. Maria Santos, MD
NPI: 1122334455
Specialty: Family Medicine

---
CHIEF COMPLAINT
Patient presents for routine 3-month follow-up of hypertension and diabetes management.

SUBJECTIVE
Mr. Anderson is a 64-year-old male presenting for quarterly chronic disease management. He reports blood pressure has been running higher at home this past month, averaging 148/90 on home cuff. Blood sugars fasting have been 130-160 mg/dL, up from previous range. He has been less adherent to low-sodium diet due to work stress. Denies chest pain, shortness of breath, headache, visual changes, polyuria, or polydipsia. Reports some fatigue. No medication side effects noted.

OBJECTIVE
Vitals:
  BP: 152/94 mmHg   HR: 78 bpm   Temp: 98.4°F   RR: 14 breaths/min
  SpO2: 98% on room air   Weight: 212 lbs

Physical Exam: Alert and oriented x3. No acute distress. Cardiovascular: RRR, no murmurs. Pulmonary: CTA bilaterally. Abdomen: Soft, non-tender. Extremities: No edema. No diabetic foot ulcers noted.

Labs reviewed (drawn 10/20/2025):
  HbA1c: 8.1% (prev 7.6% in July)
  BMP: Cr 1.1, K+ 4.2, eGFR 67
  LDL: 112 mg/dL (on statin)

ASSESSMENT
1. Essential hypertension (I10) — Suboptimally controlled. BP elevated today and per home monitoring. Contributing factor: dietary nonadherence and increased stress.
2. Type 2 diabetes mellitus without complications (E11.9) — Worsening glycemic control, HbA1c up to 8.1%.
3. Hyperlipidemia (E78.5) — LDL borderline on current statin, stable.

PLAN
1. Hypertension: Increase lisinopril from 10 mg to 20 mg daily. Reinforce low-sodium diet counseling. Home BP log reviewed.
2. Diabetes: Increase metformin to 1000 mg twice daily. Refer to diabetes education program. Recheck HbA1c in 3 months.
3. Hyperlipidemia: Continue atorvastatin 40 mg. Recheck fasting lipids in 6 months.
4. Ordered: Urine microalbumin, repeat BMP in 4 weeks.
5. Follow-up: Return to clinic in 3 months or sooner if BP > 160/100 or blood sugars consistently > 250 mg/dL.

Electronically signed: Dr. Maria Santos, MD — 2025-10-22 14:32
